name: Barbarian
description: A fierce warrior of primitive background who can enter a battle rage.

1.name: Rage
1.description: In battle, you fight with primal ferocity. On your turn, you can enter a rage as a bonus action. While raging, you gain the following benefits if you aren't wearing heavy armor: advantage on STR checks and STR saving throws; when you make a melee weapon attack using STR, you gain a bonus to the damage roll; you have resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. Your rage lasts for 1 minute. It ends early if you are knocked unconscious or if your turn ends and you haven't attacked a hostile creature since your last turn or taken damage since then. You can also end your rage on your turn as a bonus action. Once you have raged the number of times shown for your barbarian level in the Rages column of the Barbarian table, you must finish a long rest before you can rage again.

1.name: Unarmored Defense
1.description: While you are not wearing any armor, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.

2.name: Reckless Attack
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can throw aside all concern for defense to attack with fierce desperation. When you make your first attack on your turn, you can decide to attack recklessly. Doing so gives you advantage on melee weapon attack rolls using Strength during this turn, but attack rolls against you have advantage until your next turn.

2.name: Danger Sense
2.description: At 2nd level, you gain an uncanny sense of when things nearby aren't as they should be, giving you an edge when you dodge away from danger. You have advantage on Dexterity saving throws against effects that you can see, such as traps and spells. To gain this benefit, you can't be blinded, deafened, or incapacitated.

3.name: Primal Path
3.description: At 3rd level, you choose a path that shapes the nature of your rage. Your choice grants you features at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 14th levels.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

5.name: Fast Movement
5.description: Starting at 5th level, your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't wearing heavy armor.

6.name: Path Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Primal Path.

7.name: Feral Instinct
7.description: By 7th level, your instincts are so honed that you have advantage on initiative rolls. Additionally, if you are surprised at the beginning of combat and aren't incapacitated, you can act normally on your first turn, but only if you enter your rage before doing anything else on that turn.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1.

9.name: Brutal Critical
9.description: Beginning at 9th level, you can roll one additional weapon damage die when determining the extra damage for a critical hit with a melee attack. This increases to two additional dice at 13th level and three additional dice at 17th level.

10.name: Path Feature
10.description: At 10th level, you gain a feature from your Primal Path.

11.name: Relentless Rage
11.description: Starting at 11th level, your rage can keep you fighting despite grievous wounds. If you drop to 0 hit points while you're raging and don't die outright, you can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. If you succeed, you drop to 1 hit point instead.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1.

14.name: Path Feature
14.description: At 14th level, you gain a feature from your Primal Path.

15.name: Persistent Rage
15.description: Beginning at 15th level, your rage is so fierce that it ends early only if you fall unconscious or if you choose to end it.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1.

17.name: Brutal Critical (3 dice)
17.description: At 17th level, Brutal Critical now allows three additional weapon damage dice on a critical hit.

18.name: Indomitable Might
18.description: Beginning at 18th level, if your total for a Strength check is less than your Strength score, you can use that score in place of the total.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1.

20.name: Primal Champion
20.description: At 20th level, you embody the power of the wilds. Your Strength and Constitution scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.

name: Bard
description: An inspiring magician whose power echoes the music of creation.

1.name: Bardic Inspiration
1.description: You can inspire others through stirring words or music. To do so, you use a bonus action on your turn to choose one creature other than yourself within 60 feet of you who can hear you. That creature gains one Bardic Inspiration die, a d6. Once within the next 10 minutes, the creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes. The creature can wait until after it rolls the d20 before deciding to use the Bardic Inspiration die, but must decide before the DM says whether the roll succeeds or fails. Once the Bardic Inspiration die is rolled, it is lost. A creature can have only one Bardic Inspiration die at a time. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: You have learned to untangle and reshape the fabric of reality in harmony with your wishes and music. Your spells are part of your vast repertoire, magic that you can tune to different situations.

2.name: Jack of All Trades
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can add half your proficiency bonus, rounded down, to any ability check you make that doesn't already include your proficiency bonus.

2.name: Song of Rest
2.description: Beginning at 2nd level, you can use soothing music or oration to help revitalize your wounded allies during a short rest. If you or any friendly creatures who can hear your performance regain hit points by spending Hit Dice at the end of the short rest, each of those creatures regains an extra 1d6 hit points.

3.name: Bard College
3.description: At 3rd level, you delve into the advanced techniques of a bard college of your choice.

3.name: Expertise
3.description: At 3rd level, choose two of your skill proficiencies. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Bardic Inspiration (d8)
5.description: At 5th level, your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a d8.

5.name: Font of Inspiration
5.description: Beginning when you reach 5th level, you regain all of your expended uses of Bardic Inspiration when you finish a short or long rest.

6.name: Countercharm
6.description: At 6th level, you gain the ability to use musical notes or words of power to disrupt mind-influencing effects.

6.name: Bard College Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Bard College.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Bardic Inspiration (d10)
10.description: At 10th level, your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a d10.

10.name: Expertise
10.description: At 10th level, choose two more of your skill proficiencies for Expertise.

10.name: Magical Secrets
10.description: By 10th level, you have plundered magical knowledge from a wide spectrum of disciplines. Choose two spells from any class, including this one. A spell you choose must be of a level you can cast, as shown on the Bard table, or a cantrip.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Magical Secrets
14.description: At 14th level, choose two more spells from any class.

14.name: Bard College Feature
14.description: At 14th level, you gain a feature from your Bard College.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Magical Secrets
18.description: At 18th level, choose two more spells from any class.

18.name: Bardic Inspiration (d12)
18.description: At 18th level, your Bardic Inspiration die changes to a d12.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Superior Inspiration
20.description: At 20th level, when you roll initiative and have no uses of Bardic Inspiration left, you regain one use.

name: Cleric
description: A priestly champion who wields divine magic in service of a higher power.

1.name: Divine Domain
1.description: Choose one domain related to your deity. Your choice grants you domain spells and other features when you choose it at 1st level.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: As a conduit for divine power, you can cast cleric spells.

2.name: Channel Divinity
2.description: At 2nd level, you gain the ability to channel divine energy directly from your deity, using that energy to fuel magical effects. You start with two such effects: Turn Undead and an effect determined by your domain. When you use your Channel Divinity, you choose which effect to create. You must then finish a short or long rest to use your Channel Divinity again.

2.name: Turn Undead
2.description: As an action, you present your holy symbol and speak a prayer censuring the undead. Each undead that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must make a Wisdom saving throw. If the creature fails its saving throw, it is turned for 1 minute or until it takes any damage.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Destroy Undead
5.description: Starting at 5th level, when an undead fails its saving throw against your Turn Undead feature, the creature is instantly destroyed if its challenge rating is at or below a certain threshold.

6.name: Channel Divinity (2/rest)
6.description: At 6th level, you can use your Channel Divinity twice between rests.

6.name: Divine Domain Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

8.name: Divine Domain Feature
8.description: At 8th level, you gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

8.name: Destroy Undead (CR 1)
8.description: At 8th level, you destroy undead of CR 1 or lower.

10.name: Divine Intervention
10.description: Beginning at 10th level, you can call on your deity to intervene on your behalf when your need is great. Imploring your deity's aid requires you to use your action. Describe the assistance you seek, and roll percentile dice. If you roll a number equal to or lower than your cleric level, your deity intervenes. The DM chooses the nature of the intervention.

11.name: Destroy Undead (CR 2)
11.description: At 11th level, you destroy undead of CR 2 or lower.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Destroy Undead (CR 3)
14.description: At 14th level, you destroy undead of CR 3 or lower.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Divine Domain Feature
17.description: At 17th level, you gain a feature from your Divine Domain.

17.name: Destroy Undead (CR 4)
17.description: At 17th level, you destroy undead of CR 4 or lower.

18.name: Channel Divinity (3/rest)
18.description: At 18th level, you can use your Channel Divinity three times between rests.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Divine Intervention Improvement
20.description: At 20th level, your call for intervention succeeds automatically, no roll required.

name: Fighter
description: A master of martial combat, skilled with a variety of weapons and armor.

1.name: Fighting Style
1.description: You adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options: Archery (+2 to ranged attack rolls), Defense (+1 AC when wearing armor), Dueling (+2 damage with one-handed weapon and no other weapons), Great Weapon Fighting (reroll 1s and 2s on damage dice with two-handed weapons), Protection (impose disadvantage on attacks against adjacent ally), Two-Weapon Fighting (add ability modifier to off-hand attack damage).

1.name: Second Wind
1.description: You have a limited well of stamina that you can draw on to protect yourself from harm. On your turn, you can use a bonus action to regain hit points equal to 1d10 + your fighter level. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again.

2.name: Action Surge
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can push yourself beyond your normal limits for a moment. On your turn, you can take one additional action. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest before you can use it again. Starting at 17th level, you can use it twice before a rest, but only once on the same turn.

3.name: Martial Archetype
3.description: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you strive to emulate in your combat styles and techniques.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

6.name: Ability Score Improvement
6.description: When you reach 6th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

7.name: Martial Archetype Feature
7.description: At 7th level, you gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

9.name: Indomitable
9.description: Beginning at 9th level, you can reroll a saving throw that you fail. If you do so, you must use the new roll, and you can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest. You can use this feature twice between long rests starting at 13th level and three times between long rests starting at 17th level.

10.name: Martial Archetype Feature
10.description: At 10th level, you gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

11.name: Extra Attack (2)
11.description: At 11th level, you can attack three times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Ability Score Improvement
14.description: When you reach 14th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

15.name: Martial Archetype Feature
15.description: At 15th level, you gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Action Surge (2/rest)
17.description: At 17th level, you can use Action Surge twice before a rest.

18.name: Martial Archetype Feature
18.description: At 18th level, you gain a feature from your Martial Archetype.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Extra Attack (3)
20.description: At 20th level, you can attack four times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

name: Rogue
description: A scoundrel who uses stealth and trickery to overcome obstacles and enemies.

1.name: Expertise
1.description: At 1st level, choose two of your skill proficiencies, or one of your skill proficiencies and your proficiency with thieves' tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of the chosen proficiencies.

1.name: Sneak Attack
1.description: Beginning at 1st level, you know how to strike subtly and exploit a foe's distraction. Once per turn, you can deal an extra 1d6 damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll. The attack must use a finesse or a ranged weapon. You don't need advantage on the attack roll if another enemy of the target is within 5 feet of it, that enemy isn't incapacitated, and you don't have disadvantage on the attack roll. The amount of the extra damage increases as you gain levels in this class: 1d6 at 1st, 2d6 at 3rd, 3d6 at 5th, 4d6 at 7th, 5d6 at 9th, 6d6 at 11th, 7d6 at 13th, 8d6 at 15th, 9d6 at 17th, 10d6 at 19th.

1.name: Thieves' Cant
1.description: During your rogue training you learned thieves' cant, a secret mix of dialect, jargon, and code that allows you to hide messages in seemingly normal conversation.

2.name: Cunning Action
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, your quick thinking and agility allow you to move and act quickly. You can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat. This action can be used only to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

3.name: Roguish Archetype
3.description: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you emulate in the exercise of your rogue abilities.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Uncanny Dodge
5.description: Starting at 5th level, when an attacker that you can see hits you with an attack, you can use your reaction to halve the attack's damage against you.

6.name: Expertise
6.description: At 6th level, choose two more of your skill proficiencies for Expertise.

7.name: Evasion
7.description: Beginning at 7th level, you can nimbly dodge out of the way of certain area effects, such as a red dragon's fiery breath or an ice storm spell. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Dexterity saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Ability Score Improvement
10.description: When you reach 10th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

11.name: Reliable Talent
11.description: By 11th level, you have refined your chosen skills until they approach perfection. Whenever you make an ability check that lets you add your proficiency bonus, you can treat a d20 roll of 9 or lower as a 10.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Blindsense
14.description: Starting at 14th level, if you are able to hear, you are aware of the location of any hidden or invisible creature within 10 feet of you.

15.name: Slippery Mind
15.description: By 15th level, you have acquired greater mental strength. You gain proficiency in Wisdom saving throws.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Elusive
18.description: Beginning at 18th level, you are so evasive that attackers rarely gain the upper hand against you. No attack roll has advantage against you while you aren't incapacitated.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Stroke of Luck
20.description: At 20th level, you have an uncanny knack for succeeding when you need to. If your attack misses a target within range, you can turn the miss into a hit. Alternatively, if you fail an ability check, you can treat the d20 roll as a 20. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

name: Wizard
description: A scholarly magic-user capable of manipulating the structures of reality.

1.name: Arcane Recovery
1.description: You have learned to regain some of your magical energy by studying your spellbook. Once per day when you finish a short rest, you can choose expended spell slots to recover. The spell slots can have a combined level that is equal to or less than half your wizard level (rounded up), and none of the slots can be 6th level or higher.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: As a student of arcane magic, you have a spellbook containing spells that show the first glimmerings of your true power.

2.name: Arcane Tradition
2.description: When you reach 2nd level, you choose an arcane tradition, shaping your practice of magic through one of eight schools.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

6.name: Arcane Tradition Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Arcane Tradition.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Arcane Tradition Feature
10.description: At 10th level, you gain a feature from your Arcane Tradition.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Arcane Tradition Feature
14.description: At 14th level, you gain a feature from your Arcane Tradition.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Spell Mastery
18.description: At 18th level, you have achieved such mastery over certain spells that you can cast them at will. Choose a 1st-level wizard spell and a 2nd-level wizard spell that are in your spellbook. You can cast those spells at their lowest level without expending a spell slot when you have them prepared.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Signature Spells
20.description: When you reach 20th level, you gain mastery over two powerful spells and can cast them with little effort. Choose two 3rd-level wizard spells in your spellbook as your signature spells. You always have these spells prepared, they don't count against the number of spells you have prepared, and you can cast each of them once at 3rd level without expending a spell slot.

name: Ranger
description: A warrior who uses martial prowess and nature magic to combat threats on the edges of civilization.

1.name: Favored Enemy
1.description: Beginning at 1st level, you have significant experience studying, tracking, hunting, and even talking to a certain type of enemy. Choose a type of favored enemy: aberrations, beasts, celestials, constructs, dragons, elementals, fey, fiends, giants, monstrosities, oozes, plants, or undead. Alternatively, you can select two races of humanoid (such as gnolls and orcs) as favored enemies.

1.name: Natural Explorer
1.description: You are a master of navigating the natural world, and you react with swift and decisive action when attacked. This grants you several benefits when in your favored terrain type.

2.name: Fighting Style
2.description: At 2nd level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty: Archery, Defense, Dueling, or Two-Weapon Fighting.

2.name: Spellcasting
2.description: By the time you reach 2nd level, you have learned to use the magical essence of nature to cast spells, much as a druid does.

3.name: Ranger Archetype
3.description: At 3rd level, you choose an archetype that you strive to emulate.

3.name: Primeval Awareness
3.description: Beginning at 3rd level, you can use your action and expend one ranger spell slot to focus your awareness on the region around you. For 1 minute per level of the spell slot you expend, you can sense whether certain types of creatures are present within 1 mile of you.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

6.name: Favored Enemy Improvement
6.description: At 6th level, you gain another favored enemy.

6.name: Natural Explorer Improvement
6.description: At 6th level, you gain another favored terrain.

7.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
7.description: At 7th level, you gain a feature from your Ranger Archetype.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

8.name: Land's Stride
8.description: Starting at 8th level, moving through nonmagical difficult terrain costs you no extra movement. You can also pass through nonmagical plants without being slowed by them and without taking damage from them if they have thorns, spines, or a similar hazard.

10.name: Natural Explorer Improvement
10.description: At 10th level, you gain a third favored terrain.

10.name: Hide in Plain Sight
10.description: Starting at 10th level, you can spend 1 minute creating camouflage for yourself. You must have access to fresh mud, dirt, plants, soot, and other naturally occurring materials with which to create your camouflage. Once you are camouflaged in this way, you can try to hide by pressing yourself up against a solid surface, such as a tree or wall, that is at least as tall and wide as you are. You gain a +10 bonus to Dexterity (Stealth) checks as long as you remain there without moving or taking actions.

11.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
11.description: At 11th level, you gain a feature from your Ranger Archetype.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Favored Enemy Improvement
14.description: At 14th level, you gain one more favored enemy.

14.name: Vanish
14.description: Starting at 14th level, you can use the Hide action as a bonus action on your turn. Also, you can't be tracked by nonmagical means, unless you choose to leave a trail.

15.name: Ranger Archetype Feature
15.description: At 15th level, you gain a feature from your Ranger Archetype.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Feral Senses
18.description: At 18th level, you gain preternatural senses that help you fight creatures you can't see. When you attack a creature you can't see, your inability to see it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack rolls against it. You are also aware of the location of any invisible creature within 30 feet of you, provided that the creature isn't hidden from you and you aren't blinded or deafened.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Foe Slayer
20.description: At 20th level, you become an unparalleled hunter of your enemies. Once on each of your turns, you can add your Wisdom modifier to the attack roll or the damage roll of an attack you make against one of your favored enemies.

name: Paladin
description: A holy warrior bound to a sacred oath.

1.name: Divine Sense
1.description: The presence of strong evil registers on your senses like a noxious odor, and powerful good rings like heavenly music in your ears. As an action, you can open your awareness to detect such forces. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any celestial, fiend, or undead within 60 feet of you that is not behind total cover.

1.name: Lay on Hands
1.description: Your blessed touch can heal wounds. You have a pool of healing power that replenishes when you take a long rest. With that pool, you can restore a total number of hit points equal to your paladin level × 5. As an action, you can touch a creature and draw power from the pool to restore a number of hit points to that creature, up to the maximum amount remaining in your pool.

2.name: Fighting Style
2.description: At 2nd level, you adopt a style of fighting: Defense, Dueling, Great Weapon Fighting, or Protection.

2.name: Spellcasting
2.description: By 2nd level, you have learned to draw on divine magic through meditation and prayer to cast spells as a paladin.

2.name: Divine Smite
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can expend one spell slot to deal radiant damage to the target, in addition to the weapon's damage. The extra damage is 2d8 for a 1st-level spell slot, plus 1d8 for each spell level higher than 1st, to a maximum of 5d8. The damage increases by 1d8 if the target is an undead or a fiend.

3.name: Divine Health
3.description: By 3rd level, the divine magic flowing through you makes you immune to disease.

3.name: Sacred Oath
3.description: When you reach 3rd level, you swear the oath that binds you as a paladin forever. Up to this time you have been in a preparatory stage, committed to the path but not yet sworn to it. Now you choose the Oath of Devotion, the Oath of the Ancients, or the Oath of Vengeance.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice.

6.name: Aura of Protection
6.description: Starting at 6th level, whenever you or a friendly creature within 10 feet of you must make a saving throw, the creature gains a bonus to the saving throw equal to your Charisma modifier (with a minimum bonus of +1). You must be conscious to grant this bonus. At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

7.name: Sacred Oath Feature
7.description: At 7th level, you gain a feature from your Sacred Oath.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Aura of Courage
10.description: Starting at 10th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you can't be frightened while you are conscious.

11.name: Improved Divine Smite
11.description: By 11th level, you are so suffused with righteous might that all your melee weapon strikes carry divine power with them. Whenever you hit a creature with a melee weapon, the creature takes an extra 1d8 radiant damage.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Cleansing Touch
14.description: Beginning at 14th level, you can use your action to end one spell on yourself or on one willing creature that you touch. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once). You regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.

15.name: Sacred Oath Feature
15.description: At 15th level, you gain a feature from your Sacred Oath.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Aura Improvements
18.description: At 18th level, the range of your Aura of Protection and Aura of Courage increases to 30 feet.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Sacred Oath Feature
20.description: At 20th level, you gain a feature from your Sacred Oath.

name: Druid
description: A priest of the Old Faith, wielding the powers of nature and adopting animal forms.

1.name: Druidic
1.description: You know Druidic, the secret language of druids. You can speak the language and use it to leave hidden messages.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: Drawing on the divine essence of nature itself, you can cast spells to shape that essence to your will.

2.name: Wild Shape
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your action to magically assume the shape of a beast that you have seen before. You can use this feature twice. You regain expended uses when you finish a short or long rest. Your druid level determines the beasts you can transform into.

2.name: Druid Circle
2.description: At 2nd level, you choose to identify with a circle of druids.

4.name: Wild Shape Improvement
4.description: At 4th level, you can transform into a beast with a challenge rating as high as 1/2.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

6.name: Druid Circle Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Druid Circle.

8.name: Wild Shape Improvement
8.description: At 8th level, you can transform into a beast with a challenge rating as high as 1.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Druid Circle Feature
10.description: At 10th level, you gain a feature from your Druid Circle.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Druid Circle Feature
14.description: At 14th level, you gain a feature from your Druid Circle.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

18.name: Timeless Body
18.description: Starting at 18th level, the primal magic that you wield causes you to age more slowly. For every 10 years that pass, your body ages only 1 year.

18.name: Beast Spells
18.description: Beginning at 18th level, you can cast many of your druid spells in any shape you assume using Wild Shape.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Archdruid
20.description: At 20th level, you can use your Wild Shape an unlimited number of times. Additionally, you can ignore the verbal and somatic components of your druid spells, as well as any material components that lack a cost and aren't consumed by a spell.

name: Monk
description: A master of martial arts, harnessing the power of the body in pursuit of physical and spiritual perfection.

1.name: Unarmored Defense
1.description: Beginning at 1st level, while you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier.

1.name: Martial Arts
1.description: At 1st level, your practice of martial arts gives you mastery of combat styles that use unarmed strikes and monk weapons, which are shortswords and any simple melee weapons that don't have the two-handed or heavy property. You gain the following benefits while you are unarmed or wielding only monk weapons and you aren't wearing armor or wielding a shield: You can use DEX instead of STR for attack and damage rolls. You can roll a d4 in place of the normal damage of your unarmed strike or monk weapon. When you use the Attack action with an unarmed strike or a monk weapon on your turn, you can make one unarmed strike as a bonus action.

2.name: Ki
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, your training allows you to harness the mystic energy of ki. Your access to this energy is represented by a number of ki points. Your monk level determines the number of points you have. You can spend these points to fuel various ki features.

2.name: Unarmored Movement
2.description: Starting at 2nd level, your speed increases by 10 feet while you are not wearing armor or wielding a shield. This bonus increases when you reach certain monk levels.

3.name: Monastic Tradition
3.description: When you reach 3rd level, you commit yourself to a monastic tradition.

3.name: Deflect Missiles
3.description: Starting at 3rd level, you can use your reaction to deflect or catch the missile when you are hit by a ranged weapon attack.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

4.name: Slow Fall
4.description: Beginning at 4th level, you can use your reaction when you fall to reduce any falling damage you take by an amount equal to five times your monk level.

5.name: Extra Attack
5.description: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice.

5.name: Stunning Strike
5.description: Starting at 5th level, you can interfere with the flow of ki in an opponent's body. When you hit another creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 1 ki point to attempt a stunning strike.

6.name: Ki-Empowered Strikes
6.description: Starting at 6th level, your unarmed strikes count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

6.name: Monastic Tradition Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Monastic Tradition.

7.name: Evasion
7.description: At 7th level, your instinctive agility lets you dodge out of the way of certain area effects, such as a blue dragon's lightning breath or a fireball spell.

7.name: Stillness of Mind
7.description: Starting at 7th level, you can use your action to end one effect on yourself that is causing you to be charmed or frightened.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

9.name: Unarmored Movement Improvement
9.description: At 9th level, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move.

10.name: Purity of Body
10.description: At 10th level, your mastery of the ki flowing through you makes you immune to disease and poison.

11.name: Monastic Tradition Feature
11.description: At 11th level, you gain a feature from your Monastic Tradition.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

13.name: Tongue of the Sun and Moon
13.description: Starting at 13th level, you learn to touch the ki of other minds so that you understand all spoken languages.

14.name: Diamond Soul
14.description: Beginning at 14th level, your mastery of ki grants you proficiency in all saving throws. Additionally, whenever you make a saving throw and fail, you can spend 1 ki point to reroll it and take the second result.

15.name: Timeless Body
15.description: At 15th level, your ki sustains you so that you suffer none of the frailty of old age, and you can't be aged magically.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Monastic Tradition Feature
17.description: At 17th level, you gain a feature from your Monastic Tradition.

18.name: Empty Body
18.description: Beginning at 18th level, you can use your action to spend 4 ki points to become invisible for 1 minute. During that time, you also have resistance to all damage but force damage.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Perfect Self
20.description: At 20th level, when you roll for initiative and have no ki points remaining, you regain 4 ki points.

name: Sorcerer
description: A spellcaster who draws on inherent magic from a gift or bloodline.

1.name: Sorcerous Origin
1.description: Choose a sorcerous origin, which describes the source of your innate magical power.

1.name: Spellcasting
1.description: An event in your past, or in the life of a parent or ancestor, left an indelible mark on you, infusing you with arcane magic.

2.name: Font of Magic
2.description: At 2nd level, you tap into a deep wellspring of magic within yourself. This wellspring is represented by sorcery points, which allow you to create a variety of magical effects.

3.name: Metamagic
3.description: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to twist your spells to suit your needs. You gain two Metamagic options of your choice: Careful Spell, Distant Spell, Empowered Spell, Extended Spell, Heightened Spell, Quickened Spell, Subtle Spell, or Twinned Spell.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

6.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Sorcerous Origin.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

10.name: Metamagic
10.description: At 10th level, you learn one additional Metamagic option.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

14.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
14.description: At 14th level, you gain a feature from your Sorcerous Origin.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Metamagic
17.description: At 17th level, you learn one additional Metamagic option.

18.name: Sorcerous Origin Feature
18.description: At 18th level, you gain a feature from your Sorcerous Origin.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Sorcerous Restoration
20.description: At 20th level, you regain 4 expended sorcery points whenever you finish a short rest.

name: Warlock
description: A wielder of magic that is derived from a bargain with an extraplanar entity.

1.name: Otherworldly Patron
1.description: At 1st level, you have struck a bargain with an otherworldly being of your choice: the Archfey, the Fiend, or the Great Old One.

1.name: Pact Magic
1.description: Your arcane research and the magic bestowed on you by your patron have given you facility with spells. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a short or long rest.

2.name: Eldritch Invocations
2.description: In your study of occult lore, you have unearthed eldritch invocations, fragments of forbidden knowledge that imbue you with an abiding magical ability. You gain two eldritch invocations of your choice.

3.name: Pact Boon
3.description: At 3rd level, your otherworldly patron bestows a gift upon you for your loyal service. You gain one of the following features of your choice: Pact of the Chain, Pact of the Blade, or Pact of the Tome.

4.name: Ability Score Improvement
4.description: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

5.name: Eldritch Invocations Improvement
5.description: At 5th level, you gain one more eldritch invocation of your choice.

6.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
6.description: At 6th level, you gain a feature from your Otherworldly Patron.

7.name: Eldritch Invocations Improvement
7.description: At 7th level, you gain one more eldritch invocation.

8.name: Ability Score Improvement
8.description: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

9.name: Eldritch Invocations Improvement
9.description: At 9th level, you gain one more eldritch invocation.

10.name: Otherworldly Patron Feature
10.description: At 10th level, you gain a feature from your Otherworldly Patron.

11.name: Mystic Arcanum (6th level)
11.description: At 11th level, your patron bestows upon you a magical secret called an arcanum. Choose one 6th-level spell from the warlock spell list as this arcanum.

12.name: Ability Score Improvement
12.description: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

13.name: Mystic Arcanum (7th level)
13.description: At 13th level, you gain a 7th-level arcanum.

15.name: Mystic Arcanum (8th level)
15.description: At 15th level, you gain an 8th-level arcanum.

16.name: Ability Score Improvement
16.description: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

17.name: Mystic Arcanum (9th level)
17.description: At 17th level, you gain a 9th-level arcanum.

18.name: Eldritch Invocations Improvement
18.description: At 18th level, you gain one more eldritch invocation.

19.name: Ability Score Improvement
19.description: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score by 2, or two ability scores by 1.

20.name: Eldritch Master
20.description: At 20th level, you can draw on your inner reserve of mystical power while entreating your patron to regain expended spell slots. You can spend 1 minute entreating your patron for aid to regain all your expended spell slots from your Pact Magic feature. Once you regain spell slots with this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can do so again.
